text.content:
Phase 5 Carry-Forward Variables

Note the state of each variable at the start of Phase 6. The consequences below modify clocks, prices, NPC reactions, and node access.

Sereth's path

PathPhase 6 effect
A — indictedAerdy commercial contacts treat the party as adjacent to a real prosecution. Halask remains active and may be available (Vector D). Doras Kemmel finds the party plausible from the start.
B — privately removedAerdy contacts are wary. Halask cannot operate publicly. Vone reduces his quoted prices by 10% — he respects clean kinetics.
C — kinetically removedAerdy commercial contacts close to the party. The City Watch's Foreign Quarter precinct has read the Rel Astra report. Vone marks up irregular ledger access by +50%. Heat Clock starts at 1 instead of 0.

Closure motion

StatePhase 6 effect
PassedCaelith writes rarely and never with full names. Road Notes reach the party only twice in the phase. Yeomanry papers are not safe to display in Greyhawk.
Held overCaelith writes once a week. Yeomanry papers are tolerated in private but not public.
DefeatedCaelith writes whenever needed. Yeomanry papers can be displayed once without Heat Clock penalty (e.g. to gain quick access at Vone's, or to move past a Watch checkpoint).

Pell's fate

StatePhase 6 effect
ExposedThe conspiracy treats Caelith's office as fully compromised. They watch his couriers; the party's Road Note runners may be intercepted. (Roll a flat d8 each delivery; on 1, the runner is intercepted and reaches the party only with the verbal contents.)
TurnedPell sends one short message to the party midphase, via Caelith, with a fragment of conspiracy intelligence: a sender's first initial, a place name, or a date. The GM picks what is most useful.
Fed false informationThe conspiracy currently believes whichever Lie was selected (see below).
ShadowedPell continues to leak naturally. Caelith's communications are unaffected, but the party should be assumed to be observed in Greyhawk's Foreign Quarter from day three. Heat Clock starts at 1.
RemovedPell has been quietly dismissed; the leak is closed but Caelith's office is short-staffed. No effect on Phase 6 clocks. Caelith's writing is more careful out of grief.
KilledPell's death produces a Greyhawk newspaper one-line item by day two of the phase. The Foreign Quarter notices. Heat Clock starts at 1. Caelith may not write at all this phase.

Vesten Quill's outcome

StatePhase 6 effect
CapturedThe party has Kestrel & Reed's Greyhawk address from the start. Quill's deposition is available as legal leverage at K&R. Doras can be turned with Quill's deposition as the trigger.
EscapedKestrel & Reed has been warned. Penetration is harder: forgery DC +2, Doras's bribery price doubles, and the lockbox wall has been quietly cleaned of one packet. The party will know K&R is the route only by following Tassel or by Vone's irregular ledger.
Never reachedQuill is in Loftwick, untouched. He continues to move information slowly. Default state — no Phase 6 modifier.

Lies fed to the conspiracy

If the party seeded a false packet through Pell in Phase 5, the conspiracy currently believes one of the following.

LiePhase 6 effect
Lie 1 — Haskur in Rel AstraBrass Crow countersurveillance is relaxed for 6–9 days. Vone's prices drop. Korre's agents are deployed eastward. The Trail Clock advances more slowly: each visible action triggers only a 2-in-3 chance of advancement.
Lie 2 — party pursuing wood correspondentThe conspiracy turned over Aldea's cottage (Vector B opening adjusts). Greyhawk countersurveillance is unaffected. The party can still arrive in Greyhawk unnoticed.
Lie 3 — party has no usable Sereth evidenceThe Rel Astra side of the network has stood down. Halask remains protected if Vector A. Greyhawk attention is unchanged.
Lie 4 — party intends to expose Galenix NaelaxNaelax House issues a quiet retainer to a Greyhawk advocate — the advocate is corrupt and will get in the party's way at most one node. Heat Clock advances by 1 the first time the party operates publicly.
Lie 5 — party returning to HardbyKestrel & Reed's Hardby owner increases security at the Greyhawk office. Doras has been instructed to refuse all walk-ins from anyone matching the party's general description. Forgery DC +2.

Rolling all of these together

The party may have stacked variables. Apply all effects. The clearest good position is: Path A + Closure defeated + Pell turned + Quill captured + Lie 1. The clearest bad position is: Path C + Closure passed + Pell killed + Quill escaped + no Lie. The phase should still play in either case — only the difficulty and the available cleanest paths change.

Clock 1 — Haskur Trail Clock

Six segments. Tracks the conspiracy's response to the party's pressure. Each segment is a discrete change in the trail's accessibility.

SegmentState change
0 — UntouchedDefault starting state. Korre is on schedule. Haskur is at the Brass Crow upper room. Kethren's transfer is the eleventh bell two nights from now.
1 — The Brass Crow marks the party as interestedThe desk clerk and runner have been told to report. Visible repeat visits are noticed. Korre is informed by the next morning.
2 — H. V. Andren's registry entry is copied and removedThe original page is taken; a clean replacement is filed. The party can still get the entry from Wenna's memory or from Aerel's strong drawer.
3 — V. Korre changes addressThe Tannerway apartment is abandoned within twelve hours. The bakery owner does not know who left. Korre's new address is harder to recover — only Doras (if turned) or shadowing Tassel will yield it.
4 — The younger man changes courier routeKethren stops using the Brass Crow's back alley and the Tannerway side. He moves to a different cistern entrance, accessible through a Garden Quarter laundry. The eleventh-bell transfer is moved to the cistern's other side.
5 — Haskur leaves his current lodgingThe third-floor Brass Crow guest room is empty. Haskur is moved to a temporary safe room above a wine merchant on the Selintan side. Tracking him requires either Korre's testimony or a fresh witness.
6 — The Greyhawk trail goes coldHaskur is moved out of Greyhawk by Kethren before the eleventh bell. Korre flees the city. Only the Seventh Stair clue remains. The party still finishes the phase, but on a fail-forward path.

What advances Clock 1

  • Approaching the Brass Crow on day one (+1).
  • Asking after H. V. Andren by name in public (+1).
  • Threatening a clerk visibly (+1).
  • Breaking into a node and leaving signs (+1, or +2 if obvious).
  • Killing a visible agent (+2).
  • Bribing Vone too loudly (+1).
  • Using official Yeomanry authority in a public Greyhawk venue (+2).
  • Letting Korre see the same PC twice in different costumes (+1, but may be reduced if the party is also playing the Heat Clock down).

What does not advance Clock 1

  • Surveillance from the cobbler's room.
  • The Hand of Coals tavern conversation (unless Tassel goes uncaught — then +1).
  • Iren's shop (Iren never reports; she does not know what would be reported).
  • Talking to Verth.
  • Shadowing Tassel (with Stealth DC 13).
  • Doras turning (his turn is silent until the conspiracy notices, which takes longer than this phase).

Splitting the party

Splitting intelligently — one PC at the Brass Crow, one at Iren's, one at Vone's, on different days — reduces Trail Clock advancement, because no single PC produces a recognisable pattern. Splitting carelessly (two PCs at the same node on the same day, three PCs visible at three nodes simultaneously) increases advancement. Reward planning, not roll-counting.

Clock 2 — Greyhawk Heat Clock

Six segments. Measures city attention, not conspiracy attention. The two clocks advance separately; events may advance both.

SegmentState change
0 — AnonymousDefault. The party is one of a hundred unfamiliar faces in the Foreign Quarter. Cheap rooms and ordinary services are available.
1 — Foreign Quarter gossip notices the partyTwo old women on a Tinker's Way doorstep have started naming the party's general description. The barkeep at the Hand of Coals nods slightly when the party comes in. Cheap rooms are not refused; they are sometimes already let.
2 — Brass Crow warns nearby counting-housesOther Foreign Quarter counting-houses now know to watch for the party. Forgery and bribery are 25% more expensive across the quarter.
3 — City Watch asks routine questionsCaptain Jarn Heshet visits the party's lodging. He is polite, he asks where they are from and what business they have in Greyhawk. He does not press. He visits again the next day.
4 — A local fixer offers false helpAn unfamiliar man named “Reldon” (alias) approaches the party at a tavern, offering services and information. Anything they tell him is reported within an hour. Anything he sells them is partly false — the kind of false that does not get them killed but does waste a day.
5 — Edrik Vone's archive becomes harder to accessVone has had a Watch visit of his own. He doubles his prices. He no longer accepts Yeomanry papers as identification. Doras Kemmel becomes paranoid and will not turn after this point.
6 — The party must leave Greyhawk or operate under heavy scrutinyCaptain Heshet returns formally. The party is given 48 hours to wrap up their business in Greyhawk. After that, anything visible draws detention. Surveillance approaches still work; everything else is now expensive or impossible.

What advances Clock 2

  • Operating openly under Yeomanry authority (+1 each public showing).
  • Visible violence in the Foreign Quarter (+2 per incident).
  • Splitting the party in a way one of the watching parties notices (+1 per incident).
  • Visible repeat returns to the same node (+1 per visit after the first three across the phase).
  • Using forgery that is detected (+1).
  • Bribing visibly (+1 per visible bribe).
  • City-wide notice from a Greyhawk newspaper one-liner about Pell's death (Pell killed) (+1 baseline).
  • Path C carried over from Phase 5 (+1 baseline).

What does not advance Clock 2

  • Surveillance.
  • Iren's shop.
  • Tellan Verth's tavern.
  • Shadowing.
  • Quiet, single-PC visits to Vone with proper protocol.
  • Doras turning if the protection is delivered privately.

Why Heat matters more than Trail

The Trail Clock is the conspiracy responding. The Heat Clock is the city responding. The conspiracy can be outwitted; the city cannot. Greyhawk does not care about the truth, but it does care about being interrupted. If the Heat Clock reaches 5 or 6, the party is forced into a faster, harder confrontation regardless of what they have built.

Faction Reactions

Each major faction has a stable disposition until the party gives them a reason to change.

FactionDefault dispositionShifts to friendly if…Shifts to hostile if…
Brass Crow staffNeutral, fee-driven.Aerel is given protection or replacement custom; Wenna is treated kindly.Direct violence at the counting-house. Public Yeomanry authority. Heat Clock 4+.
Greyhawk City WatchIndifferent.The party arrives with a sealed Greyhawk advocate's introduction.Public violence; visible Yeomanry authority; Heat Clock 3+.
Kestrel & ReedProfessional, evasive.Doras is offered formal protection (Caelith / Aerdy advocate / Halask).Forgery detected; lockbox break-in; Quill escaped (already pre-shifted).
Edrik VoneTransactional.Coin paid quietly, blackmail material delivered, or genuine protection promised.Visible Yeomanry papers; loud bribery; visible coercion.
Korre's two agentsHostile-on-contact.Cannot be turned in this phase.(Always hostile.) Will flee before dying.
Caelith DunivarDistant ally.The party reports in via Road Notes; protects Pell or Verth; turns Doras.The party uses Yeomanry authority in ways that compromise his position (Closure passed already pre-shifts).
Cindren & Vhal residual networkQuiet, watchful.The party does not pursue C&V interests publicly.Path C carried forward; visible Aerdy interest.
Halask (Vector D only)Active legal ally.The party preserves the legal front; brings him copies of clean evidence.Path C carried forward (Halask cannot operate publicly).
Brane / Closure faction (Loftwick)Distant adversary.(Cannot shift in this phase.)Public Yeomanry authority in Greyhawk gives them rhetorical fuel back home.

Public consequences for major actions

ActionConsequence
Open violence in the Foreign Quarter+2 Heat Clock; possible detention; Foreign Quarter rooms close to the party for the rest of the phase.
Killing Haskur visibly+1 Heat Clock; legal questions in Loftwick that Caelith must answer for at least a week; the Aerdy commercial-court takes formal interest.
Capturing Haskur and surrendering him to the WatchLegal protections kick in — for Haskur. Caelith may not be able to recover him; the party loses interrogation access.
Capturing Haskur and removing him from Greyhawk privately+0 Heat in Greyhawk (no body, no question), +1 Trail Clock the next day (the conspiracy notices). Caelith handles legal cover from Loftwick.
Exposing Korre publicly+2 Heat Clock; Korre flees the city before any formal proceeding can be filed; the conspiracy's surface front in Greyhawk burns. This is satisfying and expensive.
Bribing Vone+0 Heat if quiet; +1 Heat if loud. Vone is a known quantity; bribery does not surprise the city.
Using official Yeomanry papers+1 Heat per public use. Caelith may be reprimanded if Closure passed.
Involving Greyhawk authoritiesThe Watch handles the matter on its own terms, not the party's. Heat resolves by removing the party from the case.
